# 19.2 — Auto-Generated Lesson Plans, Assessments, and Differentiation

> Implementation plan. Source: [docs/MISSING_FEATURES.md](../MISSING_FEATURES.md) §19.

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Feature ID** | 19.2 |
| **Section** | AI-Specific Capabilities |
| **Severity** | MAJOR |
| **Markets** | K12 |
| **Status (today)** | THIN |
| **Estimated effort** | L (1–2 mo) |
| **Owner (proposed)** | AI / Curriculum team |
| **Depends on** | 19.10 (Model governance), 19.11 (PII redaction), 1.2 (Skill/concept graph) |
| **Unblocks** | Instructor onboarding acceleration; K-12 curriculum standardization |

---

## 1. Problem Statement

Lextures can already generate quiz questions (`server/src/services/quiz_generation_ai.rs`), rubrics (`server/src/services/assignment_rubric_ai.rs`), and syllabus sections (`server/src/services/syllabus_section_ai.rs`). However, these are isolated point tools. An instructor entering a single learning objective cannot get a complete, coherent lesson package — lesson plan, supporting activities, differentiation for varied learners, and aligned assessment — in one workflow. K-12 teachers spend hours on lesson planning; AI generation from an objective is a primary productivity differentiator for this market.

## 2. Goals

- Allow an instructor to input a learning objective, grade level, and subject, and receive a complete lesson package: lesson plan, differentiated activity variants, and a formative assessment.
- Align generated content to a selectable standards framework (Common Core, NGSS, etc.) once the concept graph (§1.2) includes standards mappings.
- Keep humans in the loop: all generated content is editable before being published to a course.
- Extend existing AI services rather than replacing them; this is an orchestration layer over `quiz_generation_ai.rs`, `assignment_rubric_ai.rs`, and `syllabus_section_ai.rs`.

## 3. Non-Goals

- Full curriculum sequencing across an academic year (that is §1.4 adaptive paths).
- Generating video or audio instructional content (§19.5 / §19.6).
- Replacing human curriculum specialists for high-stakes assessments.
- Auto-publishing to courses without instructor review.

## 4. Personas & User Stories

- **As a 5th-grade teacher**, I want to type "Students will be able to multiply fractions" and receive a full lesson plan with a warm-up, direct instruction outline, group activity, and a 5-question exit ticket so that I can customize and teach it the next day.
- **As an instructor**, I want differentiated versions of the activity for below-grade, on-grade, and advanced students so that I can serve all learners in one preparation step.
- **As an admin**, I want all AI-generated content flagged with provenance metadata so that curriculum reviewers know it was AI-assisted.

## 5. Functional Requirements

- **FR-1.** The system MUST accept: `learning_objective` (text), `grade_level` (K–12 or HE), `subject`, optional `duration_minutes`, optional `standards_code`, and optional `differentiation_levels` (array of: below_grade, on_grade, advanced, ELL, IEP).
- **FR-2.** The system MUST orchestrate parallel calls to existing AI services to produce: lesson plan outline, one formative assessment (quiz), one activity per requested differentiation level, and a rubric for any open-ended task.
- **FR-3.** Each generated asset MUST be returned as a separate editable object (not one big blob) so the instructor can accept, edit, or reject each component individually.
- **FR-4.** The system MUST tag all generated artifacts with `{ generated_by: "lextures-ai", model_id, generation_ts }` provenance metadata (cross-ref §19.9).
- **FR-5.** The system MUST provide a "Save to Course" action that creates the lesson as a draft module with the accepted assets, not published.
- **FR-6.** The system SHOULD align generated content to the requested standards code where the concept graph has mappings; include a disclaimer when standards alignment is speculative.
- **FR-7.** The system SHOULD allow the instructor to regenerate individual components (e.g., just the quiz) without re-running the full pipeline.
- **FR-8.** The system MUST respect per-tenant model governance (§19.10) and PII redaction (§19.11) settings.

## 6. Non-Functional Requirements

- **Performance** — Full lesson package generation (all assets) MUST complete in ≤ 30 s with parallel async calls; a progress bar MUST update as each component completes.
- **Security** — Instructor role required; generated content stored per-org; cross-org content not accessible.
- **Privacy & Compliance** — Instructor-supplied objective text passes through PII proxy (§19.11); AI usage disclosed; generated content is not student data (no FERPA concern).
- **Accessibility** — Generation progress announced via aria-live; all generated text editable in accessible TipTap editor.
- **Scalability** — Lesson generation is CPU/IO-bound on LLM provider; background job pattern for long-running generation.
- **Reliability** — If one component (e.g., quiz generation) fails, return the rest with an error indicator on the failed component; allow re-try per component.
- **Observability** — `lesson_generation_requests_total`, `lesson_generation_latency_ms`, `lesson_component_failures_total{component}`.
- **Maintainability** — Orchestration in `server/src/services/lesson_plan_ai.rs`; re-use existing AI service functions.
- **Internationalization** — Generated content language follows org/instructor locale.
- **Backward compatibility** — Individual AI service endpoints unchanged.

## 7. Acceptance Criteria

- **AC-1.** *Given* a teacher submits learning objective "Identify the main idea of a passage" at grade 4, ELA, *When* generation completes, *Then* the response includes a lesson plan, an on-grade activity, and a 5-question formative quiz, all coherently themed around the same objective.
- **AC-2.** *Given* differentiation_levels includes `below_grade` and `advanced`, *When* generation completes, *Then* each variant is noticeably differentiated in vocabulary complexity and task demand.
- **AC-3.** *Given* the quiz-generation step fails with an LLM timeout, *When* the response is returned, *Then* the lesson plan and activities are present with an error indicator on the quiz slot; the instructor can retry just the quiz.
- **AC-4.** *Given* an instructor accepts all components and clicks "Save to Course", *When* the save completes, *Then* a new draft module exists in the course with all accepted assets, none published.
- **AC-5.** *Given* a generated artifact is saved, *When* the artifact is inspected via the admin API, *Then* `provenance.generated_by = "lextures-ai"` and `provenance.model_id` are present in the metadata.

## 8. Data Model

No new primary tables. Add `provenance JSONB` column to `course_structure_items` and `module_quizzes` if not present. New `lesson_generation_jobs` table (job queue pattern):

```sql
CREATE TABLE lesson_generation_jobs (
  id             UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  instructor_id  UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  course_id      UUID REFERENCES courses(id),
  input_params   JSONB NOT NULL,
  status         TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  result         JSONB,
  created_at     TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

## 9. API Surface

```
POST /api/courses/:id/lesson-generator        { objective, grade_level, subject, ... }
                                              -> 202 { job_id }
GET  /api/courses/:id/lesson-generator/:job_id -> LessonPackageDto
POST /api/courses/:id/lesson-generator/:job_id/regenerate-component { component: 'quiz'|'activity'|... }
POST /api/courses/:id/lesson-generator/:job_id/save-to-course { accepted_components: string[] }
                                              -> { module_id }
```

## 10. UI / UX

- New wizard: `clients/web/src/pages/lms/LessonGenerator.tsx` — step 1: input form; step 2: generation progress (component-by-component); step 3: review & edit each component; step 4: save to course.
- Each component renders in its natural editor (quiz in quiz editor, lesson plan in TipTap).
- "Regenerate" button on each component for individual retry.
- Provenance badge on all AI-generated content visible in the editor.

Leverage `clients/web/src/components/editor/EditorWithPrompt.tsx` for the inline editing experience.

## 11. AI / ML Considerations

- **Models**: Same as individual services; Claude-3.5-Sonnet via OpenRouter default; configurable per §19.10.
- **Orchestration**: `server/src/services/lesson_plan_ai.rs` calls `quiz_generation_ai`, `assignment_rubric_ai`, `syllabus_section_ai` concurrently using `tokio::join!`.
- **Prompt source-of-truth**: System prompts version-controlled in `lesson_generation_prompts` DB table (§19.10).
- **Eval**: Compare generated content against teacher-authored lesson plans using rubric scoring (coherence, alignment to objective, differentiation quality); RAGAS-style golden set.
- **Fallback**: If all provider calls fail, surface a clear "Generation unavailable" message with a retry button.
- **Model card**: `docs/ai/lesson-plan-model-card.md`.
- **Cost budget**: Lesson generation estimated at ~8 000 tokens; budget tracked per instructor per month (§19.14).

## 12. Integration Points

- `server/src/services/quiz_generation_ai.rs` — formative assessment generation.
- `server/src/services/assignment_rubric_ai.rs` — rubric for open-ended tasks.
- `server/src/services/syllabus_section_ai.rs` — lesson plan outline.
- §19.9 provenance — artifact signing.
- §19.10 model governance.
- §19.11 PII redaction.
- §1.2 concept graph — standards alignment.

## 13. Dependencies & Sequencing

- Must ship after: 19.11 (PII proxy), 19.10 (model governance).
- Benefits from: 1.2 (concept graph for standards alignment), but can ship without it with a disclaimer.

## 14. Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Generated content is factually wrong | H | M | Instructor-review gate before publish; prominent AI disclaimer |
| LLM costs spike from power users generating many lessons | M | M | Per-instructor monthly token budget (§19.14) |
| Standards alignment is speculative without concept graph | H | L | Disclaimer in UI when standards code provided but graph mapping absent |
| Generation latency too high (>30 s) | M | M | Parallel async calls; progressive disclosure as components stream in |

## 15. Rollout Plan

- Feature flag: `lesson_generator_enabled` (OpenFeature, default `false`).
- Phase 1: K-12 pilot teachers (hand-selected); eval harness baseline.
- Phase 2: All instructor accounts with explicit opt-in.
- Phase 3: GA.

## 16. Test Plan

- **Unit** — Input validation; parallel orchestration timeout handling; provenance tag injection.
- **Integration** — Full pipeline: input → job queued → all components generated → save to course → verify draft module.
- **End-to-end** — Playwright: instructor submits objective → waits for components → edits quiz question → saves to course.
- **AI eval** — 20 golden lesson objectives across subjects/grades; assess coherence, differentiation quality, assessment alignment.
- **Performance** — p95 full generation ≤ 30 s under 20 concurrent requests.

## 17. Documentation & Training

- Help center: "AI Lesson Generator" — walkthrough, tips for writing good objectives, editing generated content.
- AI model card: `docs/ai/lesson-plan-model-card.md`.
- Internal runbook: job queue monitoring for generation jobs.

## 18. Open Questions

1. Should we use a single large LLM call for cohesion, or parallel calls for speed? (Parallel is faster but less cohesive.)
2. Should generated content quality be scored before returning to the instructor (filter low-quality outputs)?
3. How do we handle copyright risk if the LLM paraphrases copyrighted lesson content?

## 19. References

- Existing files: `server/src/services/quiz_generation_ai.rs`, `server/src/services/assignment_rubric_ai.rs`, `server/src/services/syllabus_section_ai.rs`, `clients/web/src/components/editor/EditorWithPrompt.tsx`.
- NIST AI RMF 1.0 — Govern, Map, Measure, Manage framework.
- ISO/IEC 23894:2023 — AI risk management.
- OECD AI Principles — transparency, accountability.
- Related plans: `./19.9-ai-content-provenance.md`, `./19.10-model-governance.md`, `./19.13-eval-harness.md`.
